title: gh#67: R3.2 — Switch api/profile.service to TokenVault for seal direction
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/67.
Backlog section: Candidate 3 — TokenVault.

## Context

Second PR in Candidate 3. Migrates the API's connect-flow encryption calls to the vault. Smallest behavioral change in the series — just swaps the call shape.

## Scope

- Modify `packages/api/src/services/profile.service.ts`:
  - Inject `vault: TokenVault` via service-factory pattern (or pass as param to the connect functions, editorial call)
  - Replace the four inline `encrypt(credentials.X, encryptionKey, 1)` calls (line ~172–175) with `vault.sealTwitterCredentials({ ... })`
  - Replace the OAuth 2.0 `encrypt` calls in the LinkedIn callback path (line ~427+) with `vault.sealOAuth2AccessToken(...)` and `vault.sealOAuth2RefreshToken(...)`
  - Replace the OAuth 2.0 `encrypt` calls in the Facebook callback path (line ~547+) similarly
  - Remove `process.env.ENCRYPTION_KEY` reads inside these functions — the vault is constructed once at process start and injected
- Update `packages/api/src/index.ts` (or wherever app composition happens) to construct the vault and inject it
- Update existing tests in `packages/api/src/__tests__/services/profile.test.ts` to inject a fake vault

## Out of scope

- token-refresh-worker (R3.3)
- twitter-delete service (R3.4)
- Publishers (R3.5)

## Acceptance

- `pnpm --filter @sms/api build` and test pass
- `rg "encrypt\(" packages/api/src/services/profile.service.ts` returns no matches
- `rg "process\.env\.ENCRYPTION_KEY" packages/api/src/services/profile.service.ts` returns no matches

## References

- Depends on: #66

history.reasoning: The original evidence paths still exist. packages/api/src/services/profile.service.ts still imports encrypt/validateEncryptionKey and directly reads process.env.ENCRYPTION_KEY at lines 170 and 405. It still directly calls encrypt for Twitter credentials at lines 172-175, OAuth profile creation at lines 427 and 429, and reconnect/OAuth update at lines 547 and 565. packages/api/src/index.ts only requires ENCRYPTION_KEY at startup and does not construct or inject a TokenVault into createApp/profile routes. packages/api/src/__tests__/services/profile.test.ts still mocks @sms/shared/encryption and sets process.env.ENCRYPTION_KEY rather than injecting a fake vault. A repo-wide TokenVault/seal* search found no matching implementation or usage. Typecheck passes, but the focused Vitest command could not run in this read-only sandbox because Vite tried to write packages/api/node_modules/.vite-temp. The acceptance criteria `rg "encrypt\(" packages/api/src/services/profile.service.ts` and `rg "process\.env\.ENCRYPTION_KEY" packages/api/src/services/profile.service.ts` still return matches, so the finding remains open.

The original evidence paths still exist. Current packages/api/src/services/profile.service.ts imports TokenVault, accepts a vault parameter in createProfile/createProfileFromOAuth/reconnectProfile, uses vault.sealTwitterCredentials at line 171, vault.sealOAuth2AccessToken at lines 410 and 529, and vault.sealOAuth2RefreshToken at lines 412 and 547. The acceptance searches for encrypt( and process.env.ENCRYPTION_KEY in profile.service.ts both returned no matches. packages/api/src/index.ts now constructs createTokenVault(ENCRYPTION_KEY) at line 39 and passes tokenVault into createApp at line 68; routes pass getTokenVault() into the profile service. Tests have been updated to use a mock TokenVault in the original test path and the service OAuth test path. pnpm --filter @sms/api typecheck passed. The focused Vitest command and build could not complete in this read-only sandbox because Vitest tried to write node_modules/.vite-temp and tsc build tried to write dist files, so those failures are environmental EPERM failures rather than evidence that the finding remains open.
